_comment: Held-out evaluation set (PR: classifier integrity). Written AFTER the signal lists were frozen and never used to tune them — the point is to measure how the classifier generalizes to unseen phrasing, not to be patched until it scores 100%. Includes closing-clause cases (a question that ends in an explicit instruction, which must route to execution — the user already decided). Reported numbers are honest, not marketed.

prompts.prompt: the checkout worker double-charges a card if the webhook retries — where's the race?
our nightly export sometimes drops a few thousand rows and I have no idea why

is this the idiomatic way to do it in Go? whatever, just make it compile.
